improvement: Added a fail-closed orchestration layer that collects DNS, HTTP, served-identity, and deployment-manifest observations, classifies them, binds them to an exact 40-character source commit, and emits one acceptance packet.

verification.route: isolated Node test runtime using the exact source and test bytes submitted to GitHub
covered: served manifest commit mismatch rejected even when classifier accepts
mutable or malformed source ref rejected before network collection

acceptance_rule: accepted only when retained DNS, HTTP reachability, served research identity, exact deployed commit, classifier acceptance, and packet binding all agree without contradictions
privacy_boundary: Contains only public repository identifiers, synthetic host fixtures, synthetic documentation addresses, and abstract deployment evidence. No private chat, health data, credentials, diagnosis, or treatment claims.
limits: No authenticated Cloudflare control-plane response was available.
No resolving production hostname or live served commit was proven in this run.
Isolated deterministic tests are weaker than a clean GitHub Actions checkout and live Cloudflare observation.
Deployment evidence does not establish scientific, medical, diagnostic, or treatment truth.
next_action: Wire build-hostname-evidence-packet.mjs into the Cloudflare Pages workflow immediately after Cloudflare returns the deployment URL, retain the packet as an artifact, and require accepted=true before C-001 can advance.
